publication_title: Integrated Transcriptomic Analysis Reveals Proliferative, Checkpoint-Expressing Th1-Skewed Cytotoxic Lymphocytes Under Chronic Immune Activation

biological_process_synthesis.text: The DEG profile reveals a highly activated, proliferating T/NK cell compartment characterized by strong Th1 polarization, cytotoxic effector function, and simultaneous expression of multiple immune checkpoint receptors. Interferon-gamma signaling appears central, driving antigen presentation and inflammatory amplification. A pronounced cell-cycle signature indicates clonal expansion, while metabolic reprogramming genes suggest glycolytic activation consistent with effector differentiation. The coexistence of cytotoxic mediators and exhaustion markers suggests a chronically stimulated immune environment. Together, the data support a model of sustained antigen-driven immune activation with progressive regulatory counterbalancing.

terms: Antigen processing and presentation (MHC class II)
G2/M transition of mitotic cell cycle

statements: Chronic antigen exposure is driving a hybrid effector–exhausted T cell state characterized by concurrent proliferation and checkpoint upregulation.
EZH2-mediated epigenetic remodeling contributes to sustained effector differentiation while facilitating exhaustion programming.
Metabolic rewiring toward glycolysis is required for maintenance of IFNG-producing T cells.
IFNG-driven antigen presentation amplifies a positive feedback loop sustaining local inflammation.
Dual blockade of TIGIT and PDCD1 may preferentially reinvigorate the proliferative cytotoxic subset identified in this dataset.
The co-occurrence of strong mitotic signatures with multiple exhaustion markers suggests an actively cycling exhausted lymphocyte population rather than terminally dysfunctional cells.
Simultaneous Th1 and Th17 transcriptional signatures indicate potential lineage plasticity within the activated T cell compartment.
Induction of multiple APOBEC3 family members implies a strong interferon-driven antiviral or nucleic acid editing environment.
The presence of stromal remodeling genes suggests coordinated immune–tissue interaction or fibrosis-associated immune activation.

systems: Autoimmune diseases (e.g., rheumatoid arthritis, multiple sclerosis)

summaries.summary: KEGG pathway enrichment highlights coordinated activation of T cell receptor signaling, Th1/Th17 differentiation, NK cytotoxicity, and immune checkpoint pathways. Strong enrichment of cell cycle and DNA replication pathways indicates active clonal expansion. Together, these pathways describe a proliferating, antigen-driven adaptive immune response.

GO enrichment strongly emphasizes interferon-gamma signaling, lymphocyte activation, cytotoxic effector processes, and antigen presentation. Additional enrichment of mitotic and metabolic processes supports a model of activated T cells undergoing rapid proliferation and metabolic reprogramming.

Reactome analysis underscores interferon signaling, costimulatory signaling, granzyme-mediated apoptosis, and G2/M checkpoint regulation. These interconnected pathways support a chronic immune activation model with sustained effector differentiation and regulatory feedback via checkpoint pathways.
